trades.sentiment_summary: Alamos Gold announced updated multi-year production guidance on February 10, 2026, which drove the stock up 18.2%.[1] The company also held an Investor Day signaling readiness for potential M&A opportunities while emphasizing project execution and reserve conversion as near-term catalysts.[1] Analyst sentiment remains predominantly positive, with a consensus 'Buy' rating and nine analysts recommending Buy versus three recommending Strong Buy.[2][4]

Credo Technology reported a substantial Q3 revenue beat, with preliminary guidance of $404-$408 million significantly exceeding prior guidance of $335-$345 million.[1] The company also achieved PCI-SIG compliance for its PCIe 6.0 Toucan retimer, secured a strategic patent licensing deal with 3M, and raised full-year fiscal 2026 revenue growth guidance to over 200% year-over-year.[1][3][5]

Apollo Global Management reported strong Q4 2025 earnings on February 9, beating EPS estimates with $2.47 vs. $2.04 expected and revenue of $9.86 billion far exceeding forecasts, driving a 2.6% stock rally and AUM growth to ~$938B.[1][2][3] Positive developments include a nearing $3.4B AI-related financing deal with xAI, a distribution partnership with Schroders for multi-billion-dollar flows, and involvement in deals like Clear Channel's $6.2B acquisition.[2][4] Barclays raised its price target to $158, amid analyst buy ratings and institutional buying.[1][2]

Allen Operations LLC sold 6,122 shares of Astera Labs (ALAB) on February 10, 2026, amid anticipation for the company's Q4 2025 earnings release on the same day, with expected revenue growth of 77% and EPS growth of 38% year-over-year[1][2][4]. The stock opened up 10.5% at $187.67 and was later trading higher at around $194, supported by bullish technical indicators like momentum above key moving averages and a positive MACD[1][3]. Coverage highlights strong analyst support with a 'Moderate Buy' rating, though notes stretched valuations and competition[1][2][3].

Rosenblatt raised its price target for Arista Networks (ANET) to $165 from $140 while maintaining a Neutral rating, citing significant new contract wins with Google, Anthropic, Meta, and Microsoft ahead of Q4 earnings on February 12.[2] A recent analysis highlights Arista's accelerating AI networking revenue from $1.5B in 2025 to $2.75B in 2026, projecting stock upside to $184 by 2027 despite a 16% drop from all-time highs.[1] The stock opened higher at $141.79 and showed positive performance amid Moderate Buy consensus from analysts.[3][5][6]

No news articles from the last 24 hours (since February 9, 2026, 4 PM UTC) were found in the search results regarding Eastern Bankshares (EBC) stock. The most recent coverage includes a 52-week high reached on February 2, 2026, with positive analyst ratings and Q4 2025 earnings beats, alongside recent price gains to $21.97 as of February 9.

Kanzhun Limited (BZ) announced that its trustee purchased 344,224 Class A shares on February 5, 2026, at $8.71 each and 335,932 shares on February 6, 2026, at $8.92 each for its Post-IPO Share Scheme, funded by internal resources. These transactions represent about 0.03-0.04% of outstanding shares and signal confidence in retaining talent. No other significant news events reported in the last 24 hours.

Amkor Technology (AMKR) reported strong Q4 2025 results on February 9, 2026, with revenue of $1.89 billion (up 15.9% YoY) and EPS of $0.69, significantly beating consensus estimates.[1][2][4][6] The company issued upbeat Q1 guidance above expectations, driven by advanced packaging demand, iOS communications, and operational improvements in Vietnam.[1] Needham raised its price target to $65, and Zacks ranks AMKR #1 (Strong Buy) amid positive industry outlook.[2][3][4]

The ASX 200 closed higher by 0.19% on February 10, 2026, driven by gains in IT, healthcare, and gold sectors despite retreats in major banks ahead of earnings.[1][3] Uranium stocks rebounded following broker upgrades to long-term price forecasts, while individual shares like CSL and Seek showed gains amid beaten-down valuations.[2] Some stocks such as Aristocrat Leisure and CAR Group are tipped for significant upside by analysts despite yearly declines.[4]

Axalta Coating Systems reported record 2025 earnings, profit, and cash flow, with a strengthened balance sheet showing net debt to EBITDA at a historic low of 2.3x.[1][4] The company announced positive 2026 guidance including low-single-digit net sales growth, Adjusted EBITDA of $1,140–$1,170 million, and free cash flow above $500 million, alongside progress on its all-stock merger of equals with AkzoNobel expected in late 2026 or early 2027.[1][4] Q4 2025 EPS slightly missed estimates at $0.59 versus $0.60 expected, with revenue down year-over-year, but overall full-year results were highlighted positively.[2][3]

Albertsons Companies (NYSE: ACI) announced a $0.15 per share common stock dividend for Q4 fiscal 2025 and priced $2.1 billion in senior notes for refinancing, expected to close around February 2, 2026. Recent stock performance shows a 2.81% daily gain to $19.77 as of mid-October 2025 data, with options market activity suggesting potential volatility and a predicted spike. Analyst sentiment remains neutral with a Zacks Rank #3 (Hold) amid downward earnings estimate revisions.

Century Aluminum (CENX) announced on February 9, 2026, that Bechtel will lead preparatory engineering for its new Oklahoma aluminum smelter project with Emirates Global Aluminium, driving a 5.92% stock price increase to $55.50[2][6]. The stock also rose 3.6% on the prior Monday amid collaboration updates on the U.S. smelter project[5]. Trading remains above key moving averages near 52-week highs with positive analyst ratings and no recent negative news[1][4].

Emerson Electric (EMR) shares reached a new 52-week high of $161.02 following multiple analyst upgrades, including Stephens raising its price target to $155, Rothschild & Co to $165 with a 'buy' rating, and others like UBS to $168 'buy' and KeyCorp to $185 'overweight'. The stock outperformed the market with +9.1% returns over the past month amid strong Q1 2026 earnings that beat EPS estimates ($1.46 vs. $1.41), robust orders growth, and positive FY2026 guidance. An insider Rule 144 sale notice was filed for ~6,703 shares on February 9, 2026, but positive management commentary highlights accelerating innovation and demand in key regions.

New February 2026 options trading for Barrick Mining Corp (NYSE:B) began, with put and call contracts showing modest premiums that present attractive entry points for buyers and covered call strategies[2]. Analyst consensus remains a Buy rating from 8 analysts, with a 2026 price target of $48.62, though no new ratings emerged in the last 24 hours[3]. No major negative news or events specific to B stock reported today.

BILL Holdings stock surged 37% following a strong quarterly earnings report on February 5, 2026, beating estimates with $0.64 EPS and $414.67M revenue, while raising FY 2026 guidance to 2.330-2.410 EPS[1][2]. Analysts upgraded targets, including Jefferies calling it a 'game changer,' Canaccord Genuity to $77, and TIKR's model projecting $74 with 50% upside[1][2][4]. Consensus remains 'Moderate Buy' with an average target of $56-62, amid mostly bullish ratings in recent months[2][3].

Equinox Gold completed the sale of its Brazil operations for up to $1.015 billion in cash, receiving $900 million at closing and using proceeds to repay $800 million in debt, reducing net debt to $150 million.[1] The stock rose 5.4% to $15.35 and gained 6.66% on February 9, 2026, approaching its 52-week high amid strong production guidance of 700,000–800,000 ounces for 2026.[2][3] Analyst consensus remains a Buy rating with a $17.00 price target.[4][5]

Datadog reported strong Q4 2025 results with revenue of $953 million, up 29% year-over-year, beating estimates, and raised FY2026 and Q1 guidance above expectations, driving a 14-16% stock surge[1][3][4][5][6]. The company highlighted growth in large customers (603 with $1M+ ARR) and new product launches like Bits AI SRE Agent[6]. While some analysts lowered price targets to $150-195, most retained 'buy' or 'outperform' ratings amid positive operational momentum[1][2][5].
